LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT YOICHI YOSHIDA'S MOTION TO SEEK LEAVE TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. MR. YOSHIDA SHALL FILE HIS PROPOSED CROSS-COMPLAINT WITHIN 10 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. CCP 426.50 HAS BEEN INTERPRETED TO REQUIRE THE COURT TO GRANT A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A COMPULSORY CROSS-COMPLAINT ABSENT A SHOWING OF BAD FAITH. (SILVER ORGANIZATIONS LTD. V. FRANK (1990) 217 CAL.APP.3D 94, 98-99.) NO SHOWING OF BAD FAITH HAS BEEN MADE HERE. THE COURT RECOGNIZES THAT MR. YOSHIDA MISSED THE DEADLINE FOR SERVING THE MOTION SET FORTH IN THE NOVEMBER 27, 2013 ORDER SHORTENING TIME BY A DAY. THE COURT FINDS, HOWEVER, THAT THE STRONG POLICY OF HEARING CASES ON THEIR MERITS OUTWEIGHS THE PREJUDICE TO WATER BUFFALO PICTURES CAUSED BY THE LATE SERVICE OF THIS MOTION. (SEE E.G., SIDNEY V. SUP. CT. (1988) 198 CAL.APP.3D 710, 718, "THIS PRINCIPLE OF LIBERALITY REQUIRES THAT A STRONG SHOWING OF BAD FAITH BE MADE IN ORDER TO SUPPORT A DENIAL OF THE RIGHT TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT'IT IS PREFERABLE THAT THE PARTIES HAVE THEIR DAY IN COURT.") THIS IS NOT A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION GOVERNED BY CCP 1008 BECAUSE THE EX PARTE MOTION WAS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BE HEARD ON A LATER DATE PURSUANT TO THE ORDER SHORTENING TIME. THE PROPOSED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS NOT BARRED ON THE FACE OF THE PLEADING BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS BECAUSE IT ALLEGES THE BREACH DID NOT OCCUR UNTIL DECEMBER 2011. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: LAURA MARTINEZ, CSR #11332

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-29-2013 Set For Trial On May-05-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seek Leave To File A X-Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CONOR GRANAHAN, ESQ., 415.830.3325, FOR CROSS-DEFENDANT YOICHI YOSHIDA; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT YOICHI YOSHIDA'S Motion To Seek Leave To File A Cross-Complaint IS Granted. Mr. Yoshida shall file his proposed cross-complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. CCP 426.50 has been interpreted to require the Court to grant a motion for leave to file a compulsory cross-complaint absent a showing of bad faith. (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99.) No showing of bad faith has been made here. The Court recognizes that Mr. Yoshida missed the deadline for serving the motion set forth in the November 27, 2013 Order Shortening Time by a day. The Court finds, however, that the strong policy of hearing cases on their merits outweighs the prejudice to Water Buffalo Pictures caused by the late service of this motion. (See e.g., Sidney v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 710, 718, "This principle of liberality requires that a strong showing of bad faith be made in order to support a denial of the right to file a cross-complaint'It is preferable that the parties have their day in court.") This is not a motion for reconsideration governed by CCP 1008 because the ex parte motion was denied without prejudice to be heard on a later date pursuant to the Order Shortening Time. The proposed cross-complaint is not barred on the face of the pleading by the statute of limitations because it alleges the breach did not occur until December 2011. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332
